sources.title: Sermorelin: a review of its use in the diagnosis and treatment of children with GH deficiency
sources.authors: Walker RF
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of sermorelin's FDA-approved use; effective for diagnosis and treatment of pediatric GHD

sources.title: GHRH and GHRP in combination: a new concept in GH therapy
sources.authors: Bowers CY et al.
sources.keyFindings: Demonstrated synergism between GHRH analogs (like sermorelin) and GHRPs; foundational for combination therapy research

sources.title: Dose-response of growth hormone to sermorelin and GHRP-2
sources.authors: Jaffe CA et al.
sources.keyFindings: Characterized GH dose-response to sermorelin; established effective dosing range

sources.title: Effects of sermorelin on sleep in healthy young adults
sources.authors: Frieboes RM et al.
sources.keyFindings: Sermorelin enhanced slow-wave sleep and GH secretion without affecting REM sleep

sources.title: Once daily subcutaneous growth hormone-releasing hormone therapy accelerates growth in growth hormone-deficient children during the first year of therapy
sources.authors: Thorner MO, Rochiccioli P, Colle M et al.
sources.keyFindings: Geref International Study: 74% of GH-deficient children showed significant growth acceleration with once-daily sermorelin

sources.title: Effect of growth hormone-releasing hormone on the somatotropic axis in normal aging and in growth hormone-deficiency states
sources.authors: Corpas E, Harman SM, Blackman MR
sources.keyFindings: GHRH(1-29) reversed age-related decreases in GH and IGF-1 levels in elderly men; 14-day twice-daily administration

sources.title: Long-term administration of sermorelin improves body composition and GH/IGF-1 levels in healthy elderly
sources.authors: Khorram O, Laughlin GA, Yen SS
sources.keyFindings: 5-month daily GHRH treatment increased nocturnal GH, serum IGF-1; improvements in insulin sensitivity, libido, and quality of life in men

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone effects on brain GABA levels in mild cognitive impairment and healthy aging
sources.authors: Friedman SD, Baker LD, Borber S et al.
sources.keyFindings: 20-week GHRH increased brain GABA levels; improved working memory, planning, selective attention, processing speed

sources.title: Effects of human pancreatic growth hormone releasing factor on growth hormone secretion in young and old normal adults
sources.authors: Iovino M, Monteleone P, Steardo L
sources.keyFindings: Demonstrated preserved pituitary responsiveness to GHRH(1-29) in aging; age-related decline is hypothalamic, not pituitary

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH 1-29) stimulates the somatotrope axis in elderly men
sources.authors: Lang I, Schernthaner G, Pietschmann P et al.
sources.keyFindings: GHRH(1-29) effectively stimulates GH release in elderly; response magnitude correlates with baseline IGF-1

sources.title: Acute and chronic effects of GHRH(1-29)NH2 on GH secretion in normal elderly and young adults
sources.authors: Shibasaki T, Shizume K, Nakahara M et al.
sources.keyFindings: Chronic GHRH administration increases IGF-1 in elderly; demonstrates sustained efficacy without tachyphylaxis

sources.title: Growth hormone response to growth hormone-releasing hormone in children with GH deficiency
sources.authors: Cacciari E, Zucchini S, Carla G et al.
sources.keyFindings: Established GHRH test diagnostic criteria; distinguished hypothalamic from pituitary GH deficiency

sources.title: Growth hormone releasing factor (GRF 1-29) in depression and anorexia nervosa
sources.authors: Rolla M, Andreoni A, Belliti D et al.
sources.keyFindings: Abnormal GH response to GHRH in depression; preserved response in anorexia nervosa

sources.title: Growth hormone responses to GHRH 1-29 in normal and type 1 diabetic subjects
sources.authors: Press M, Notarganelo HJ, Ames D et al.
sources.keyFindings: Altered GH response pattern in Type 1 diabetes; implications for diabetic growth retardation

sources.title: Growth hormone releasing factor: direct effects on growth hormone, glucose, and behavior via the brain
sources.authors: Vaccarino FJ, Bloom FE, Rivier J et al.
sources.keyFindings: Foundational work on GHRH(1-29) mechanism; established CNS effects independent of peripheral GH

sources.title: Characterization of growth hormone releasing factor from a human pancreatic islet tumor
sources.authors: Guillemin R, Brazeau P, Böhlen P et al.
sources.keyFindings: Original discovery and characterization of GHRH; identified 1-29 as biologically active fragment (sermorelin)

sources.title: A hypothalamic peptide that releases growth hormone
sources.authors: Rivier J, Spiess J, Thorner MO et al.
sources.keyFindings: Parallel discovery confirming GHRH structure; established foundation for sermorelin development

sources.title: Growth hormone releasing factor treatment of pituitary dwarfism
sources.authors: Ross RJ, Borges F, Grossman A et al.
sources.keyFindings: Early proof-of-concept for GHRH therapy in GH deficiency; showed growth acceleration

sources.title: Treating age-related changes in somatotrophic hormones, sleep, and cognition
sources.authors: Vitiello MV, Moe KE, Merriam GR et al.
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of GHRH effects on sleep architecture and cognitive function in aging

sources.title: Sermorelin: a better approach to management of adult-onset growth hormone insufficiency?
sources.authors: Walker RF
sources.keyFindings: Argues for sermorelin over GH replacement in adult-onset insufficiency; discusses physiologic advantages

sources.title: Growth hormone secretagogues and growth hormone releasing peptides act at different sites
sources.authors: Ghigo E, Arvat E, Muccioli G et al.
sources.keyFindings: Demonstrated distinct mechanisms of GHRH vs GHRPs; confirmed additive/synergistic effects

sources.title: GHRH plus arginine as a diagnostic test for GH deficiency in adults
sources.authors: Aimaretti G, Corneli G, Razzore P et al.
sources.keyFindings: GHRH+arginine test validation; established as gold standard diagnostic in adults

sources.title: GH-releasing effect of the synthetic peptide GHRP-6 and GHRH in peripubertal patients with GH neurosecretory dysfunction
sources.authors: Mericq V, Cassorla F, Garcia H et al.
sources.population: Peripubertal children with GH neurosecretory dysfunction
sources.keyFindings: GHRH testing distinguishes hypothalamic from pituitary dysfunction; diagnostic utility confirmed

sources.title: Effects of eight months treatment with graded doses of a growth hormone (GH)-releasing hormone in GH-deficient adults
sources.authors: Vittone J, Blackman MR, Busby-Whitehead J et al.
sources.keyFindings: 8-month GHRH therapy dose-response; sustained efficacy and safety in adults

sources.title: Body composition and bone mineral density in growth hormone-deficient adults: effects of GH and GHRH
sources.authors: Johannsson G, Svensson J, Bengtsson BA
sources.keyFindings: Comparative effects of GH vs GHRH on body composition and bone; GHRH may have advantages for long-term use

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone: not only a neurohormone
sources.authors: Granata R, Broglio F, Migliorino D et al.
sources.keyFindings: GHRH actions beyond pituitary; cardiovascular, immune, and metabolic effects documented

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone in aging
sources.authors: Muller EE, Locatelli V, Cocchi D
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of somatopause; GHRH as intervention for age-related GH decline

sources.title: Growth hormone secretagogues: history, mechanism of action, and clinical development
sources.authors: Bowers CY
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive history of GHRH and GHS development; sermorelin's role in establishing proof-of-concept

sources.title: Effects of six months of growth hormone-releasing hormone in older men and women
sources.authors: Merriam GR, Schwartz RS, Vitiello MV
sources.keyFindings: 6-month GHRH increased IGF-1 and lean mass; fat mass decreased; no major adverse effects

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone treatment effects on sleep, cognition, and quality of life in healthy older adults
sources.authors: Vitiello MV, Moe KE, Merriam GR et al.
sources.journal: Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
sources.keyFindings: GHRH improved sleep quality and cognitive function; enhanced quality of life measures

sources.title: Effects of GHRH(1-29) on sleep and pituitary hormone secretion in rats
sources.authors: Obal F Jr, Alfoldi P, Cady AB et al.
sources.keyFindings: GHRH promotes slow-wave sleep via CNS mechanisms independent of GH release

sources.title: GHRH induces slow wave sleep in normal subjects
sources.authors: Marshall L, Molle M, Boschen G et al.
sources.keyFindings: GHRH administration increased slow-wave sleep and decreased wakefulness

sources.title: Neuroendocrine control of growth hormone secretion during sleep in humans
sources.authors: Steiger A, Herth T, Holsboer F
sources.keyFindings: Detailed analysis of GHRH-somatostatin interaction in sleep-related GH secretion

sources.title: Growth hormone releasing factor (GRF 1-29) in organic brain impairment
sources.authors: Steiger A, Holsboer F
sources.keyFindings: GH response to GHRH preserved in most organic brain disorders; utility as diagnostic tool

sources.title: Effects of GHRH on GH secretion in HIV-infected patients with lipodystrophy
sources.authors: Mauss S, Wolf E, Jaeger H
sources.keyFindings: GHRH testing revealed GH axis abnormalities in HIV lipodystrophy; precursor to tesamorelin research

sources.title: Growth hormone response to growth hormone-releasing hormone in the USSR
sources.authors: Dedov II, Tikhonov VV, Goncharov NP
sources.keyFindings: Soviet research confirming GHRH diagnostic utility; population-specific response data

sources.title: GH response to GHRH(1-29) in short children with different growth patterns
sources.authors: Wiedemann E, Schwartz E
sources.keyFindings: GHRH testing discriminates between different etiologies of short stature

sources.title: Growth hormone releasing hormone in children with growth hormone deficiency: a multicenter European study
sources.authors: De Zegher F, Vanderschueren-Lodeweyckx M, Spitz B et al.
sources.keyFindings: Large European multicenter validation of GHRH diagnostic testing; established European reference ranges

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone as a therapy for brain repair: does it work?
sources.authors: Devesa J, Almenglo C, Devesa P
sources.keyFindings: Emerging evidence for GHRH neuroprotective effects; mechanism via local GH/IGF-1 and direct actions

sources.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone in the regulation of sleep and arousal
sources.authors: Szentirmai E, Kapas L, Krueger JM
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of GHRH role in sleep; direct CNS actions via hypothalamic circuits

fdaHistory.reason: Commercial decision (low sales), not safety concerns

europe.notes: Marketed in several EU countries; withdrawn for commercial reasons similar to US

japan.notes: Extensive Japanese research on GHRH; not commercially marketed

researchCenters.contributions: GHRH/GHRP interaction studies, diagnostic test validation (Ghigo, Aimaretti)
Sleep and neuroendocrine studies (Steiger, Holsboer, Marshall)
Somatopause and GHRH in aging (Muller, Locatelli)
Original GHRH discovery and characterization (Guillemin, Rivier)

notes: Sermorelin was FDA-approved in 1997 for pediatric GH deficiency and as a diagnostic agent. Discontinued commercially in 2008 for business reasons. Remains available through compounding pharmacies. Extensive global research base from US, Europe, Japan, and Russia/USSR. Foundation for newer GHRH analogs (tesamorelin, CJC-1295).
